title: Introduction to mdJSONdictio
text: Translates data frame tabular data dictionary list object can subsequently converted mdJSON imported mdEditor Dictionary Record. input data dictionary must formatted template described Tabular Data Dictionary Template article. required parameter title, accepts string designate title Dictionary Record mdEditor.

title: Introduction to mdJSONdictio
text: Translates list object converted mdEditor mdJSON data dictionary file data frame used human-readable reference. output formatted template described [Tabular Data Dictionary Template] section. Optional parameters entity_num dictionary_num, accept integers indicate dictionary entity processed one exists mdJSON file. useful multiple entities created Entity tab Dictionary Record multiple dictionaries exported together mdEditor. default, entity_num = 1 dictionary_num = 1, mdJSON file one entity dictionary, parameters can ignored. Note: Depending Dictionary Record completed mdEditor, mdJSON file processed function may output table additional columns tabular data dictionary template. Refer mdJSON Data Dictionary translate mdEditor terms.

title: Introduction to mdJSONdictio
text: Compares mdJSON data dictionary dataset summarizes discrepancies data frame. Optional parameters entity_num dictionary_num, accept integers indicate dictionary entity processed one exists mdJSON file. useful multiple entities created Entity tab Dictionary Record multiple dictionaries exported together mdEditor. default, entity_num = 1 dictionary_num = 1, mdJSON file one entity dictionary, parameters can ignored.

what: mdJSON data dictionary imported as a list in R

                                                                                                              #> 20    20 SpeciesCode       domainItem_value "Dataset variable contains entry value(s) not listed under \"domainItem_Value\" in dictionary: Aircraft noise is pervasive across the USA, including in national parks, but its effects on wildlife remain unresolved. As with other noise sources, aircraft noise may affect species physiology and behaviour by being perceived as a threat, distracting individuals, or degrading the sensory environment. This study aimed to understand the effect of aircraft traffic and associated noise on the richness of bird vocalization activity in a remote national park in the USA. We used a continent-wide acoustic dataset encompassing over 30:00 h of annotated recordings to identify two geographically similar sites with high rates of bird vocalizations and both high and low rates of aircraft noise. We selected sites in Denali National Park, both of which experience little human presence, and quantified the richness of bird vocalizations before, during and after aircraft events. We present evidence of a community-level behavioural response to aircraft noise, with increased bird vocalization richness after aircraft events at a site with relatively lower aircraft noise. At the site with low rates of aircraft noise, we found bird vocalization richness did not significantly change during an aircraft event but did increase after an aircraft event. At the site with high rates of aircraft noise, bird vocalization richness did not significantly change during or after an aircraft event. This study provides new insights into wildlife responses to aircraft traffic and associated noise and highlights the importance of noise research in the management of relatively quiet and undisturbed landscapes. , BLPW, SPSA, UPSA, REKN, GRYE, LEYE, BALO, SBDO, SEOW, SOSA" #> 21    21 BBLStatus         domainItem_value "Dataset variable contains entry value(s) not listed under \"domainItem_Value\" text: Compares tabular data dictionary dataset summarizes discrepancies data frame. input data dictionary must formatted template described Tabular Data Dictionary Template article.

                                                                                                                                                                              #> 17    17 SpeciesCode       domainItem_value "Dataset variable contains entry value(s) not listed under \"domainItem_Value\" in dictionary: Aircraft noise is pervasive across the USA, including in national parks, but its effects on wildlife remain unresolved. As with other noise sources, aircraft noise may affect species physiology and behaviour by being perceived as a threat, distracting individuals, or degrading the sensory environment. This study aimed to understand the effect of aircraft traffic and associated noise on the richness of bird vocalization activity in a remote national park in the USA. We used a continent-wide acoustic dataset encompassing over 30:00 h of annotated recordings to identify two geographically similar sites with high rates of bird vocalizations and both high and low rates of aircraft noise. We selected sites in Denali National Park, both of which experience little human presence, and quantified the richness of bird vocalizations before, during and after aircraft events. We present evidence of a community-level behavioural response to aircraft noise, with increased bird vocalization richness after aircraft events at a site with relatively lower aircraft noise. At the site with low rates of aircraft noise, we found bird vocalization richness did not significantly change during an aircraft event but did increase after an aircraft event. At the site with high rates of aircraft noise, bird vocalization richness did not significantly change during or after an aircraft event. This study provides new insights into wildlife responses to aircraft traffic and associated noise and highlights the importance of noise research in the management of relatively quiet and undisturbed title: Introduction to mdJSONdictio
text: mdJSONdictio functions intended anyone writes mdEditor metadata. build.mdJSON() helps ease process creating mdEditor Dictionary Records allowing users complete fields simplified tabular format. build.table() creates human-readable data dictionary reference mdEditor Dictionary Record. potential workflow use build.mdJSON() initiate Dictionary Record, mdEditor web application maintain Dictionary Record, build.table() create --date human-readable data dictionary needed. validate.mdJSON() validate.table() can used verify whether mdJSON tabular data dictionary accurately represents dataset.

title: Introduction to mdJSONdictio
text: Hands-Programming R mdEditor User Manual mdJSON Data Dictionary mdCodes Viewer

title: Tabular Data Dictionary Template
text: two main components tabular data dictionary template attributes domain items, represented row. Attributes units information (metadata) describing data fields (e.g. columns table). Domain items accepted entry values data field, collectively make domain attribute. important remember attribute domains defined. example, attribute “Sex” may domain comprised three domain items (“F”,“M”, “U”), attribute “Year” defined domain since unlimited number years can entered.  table describes header tabular data dictionary template, represent field(s) mdEditor Dictionary Records. “Definition” describes template column. definitions derived mdEditor User Manual. “Type” indicates type value allowed template column. “Required” indicates whether template column required attributes /domain items (“” indicates required either attributes domain items). Note: template columns “codeName,” “domainItem_name,” “domainItem_value,” “definition” required attributes domain items. template columns “dataType” “allowNull” required attributes

title: Tabular Data Dictionary Template
text: Attributes require defined data type. “dataType” template column accepts numerous values shown table . derived mdEditor web application, definitions acquired mdCodes Viewer mdTools interface.

title: How to Setup mdJSONdictio
text: Note: instructions apply computer system R RStudio.

title: How to Setup mdJSONdictio
text: Paste following RStudio Source pane run line code. Run code chunks separately ensure successfully executed.
code: # Install devtools from CRAN install.packages("devtools")  # Install mdJSONdictio from GitHub devtools::install_github("hdvincelette/mdJSONdictio", ref = 'v0.0.1')

title: How to Setup mdJSONdictio
text: mdJSONdictio requires packages work properly. R automatically install dependent packages currently library installation. R may also prompt update dependent packages already library. update packages, type “1” Console pane hit Enter. pop-asks “want install sources packages need compilation?”, click Yes.

title: How to Setup mdJSONdictio
text: dependent package successfully installed updated, mdJSONdictio installation complete R return “DONE” message.

title: How to Setup mdJSONdictio
text: Run following code verify mdJSONdictio successfully installed. R simply prints executed code Console pane, installation successful. R returns Error message “package called ‘mdJSONdictio’,” installation failed. See Troubleshooting Tips help.
code: library(mdJSONdictio)

title: How to Setup mdJSONdictio
text: Note: instructions apply computer system R RStudio. Package installation can fickle process, particularly using GitHub. package fails install, R returns Warning Error messages often descriptive intuitive enough understand cause failure. frequently, mdJSONdictio installation fails dependent packages installed updated, command line settings unsuitable. Solutions issues described . sure current problem , work solution mdJSONdictio installs successfully.

previous_headings: Troubleshooting tips > Failed installation due to dependent packages
title: How to Setup mdJSONdictio
text: Paste following RStudio Source pane run line code. see message “dependent packages installed”, rerun mdJSONdictio installation code. see message “Error: One dependent packages install”, install listed packages manually described Solution 2 .
code: # Create list of dependent packages packages<-c(   "purrr",   "readxl",   "rjson",   "stats",   "tibble",   "stringr",   "uuid",   "dplyr",   "plyr" )  # Install/re-install packages installed_packages <- packages %in% rownames(installed.packages()) if (any(installed_packages == FALSE)) {   install.packages(packages[!installed_packages]) }  # Check all packages installed installed_packages <- packages %in% rownames(installed.packages()) if ((all(installed_packages == TRUE))) {   print("All dependent packages installed") } else stop (   "One or more dependent packages did not install\n", toString(packages[installed_packages == FALSE]))

previous_headings: Troubleshooting tips > Failed installation due to dependent packages

title: mdJSONdictio Tutorial
text: Convert new list object JSON using toJSON(). create another data frame object Environment window. Export JSON using write(). Enter desired name mdJSON data dictionary file file =.
code: new.json = rjson::toJSON(x = new.list) write(x = new.json, file = "e.g.dictionary.json")

title: mdJSONdictio Tutorial
text: see build.mdJSON() works, process example tabular data dictionary mdJSON. Paste following RStudio Source pane run line code. code save JSON current working directory, recommended set working directory navigable location getting started.
code: # Load mdJSONdictio library(mdJSONdictio)  # Import tabular data dictionary as data frame path<-system.file("extdata", "e.g.dictionary.xlsx", package = "mdJSONdictio") input.table<-readxl::read_excel(path = path)  # Translate data frame to list new.list<- mdJSONdictio::build.mdJSON(x = input.table, title = "Example Dictionary")  # Convert list to JSON new.json = rjson::toJSON(x = new.list)  # Export JSON to disk write(x = new.json, file = "e.g.dictionary.json")

title: mdJSONdictio Tutorial
text: getting started, make sure prep R workspace.

title: mdJSONdictio Tutorial
text: Read JSON file R using fromJSON(). Enter file name mdJSON data dictionary x =. Make sure surround name quotes include file extension. function save JSON file list object Environment window. Select object Environment window view.
code: input.json <- rjson::fromJSON(file = "e.g.dictionary2.json")

title: mdJSONdictio Tutorial
text: Translate list object data frame using build.table(). Multiple Dictionary Records can exported together mdEditor. Indicate dictionary process dictionary_num =. one dictionary input JSON (often case), leave default value 1. one dictionary, indicate process typing integer dictionary_num =. mdEditor Dictionary Records can include one entity. Indicate entity process entity_num =. one entity dictionary (often case), leave default value 1. one entity, indicate process typing integer entity_num =. Select object Environment window view verify.
code: # Translate list to data frame new.table<- mdJSONdictio::build.table(x = input.json, dictionary_num = 1, entity_num = 1)

title: mdJSONdictio Tutorial
text: Export data frame using write(). Enter desired name tabular data dictionary file file =.
code: # Export table to disk write.csv(x = new.table, "e.g.dictionary2.csv", na="", row.names = FALSE)

title: mdJSONdictio Tutorial
text: see build.table() works, process example mdJSON data dictionary table. Paste following RStudio Source pane run line code. code save table current working directory, recommended set working directory navigable location getting started.
code: # Load mdJSONdictio library(mdJSONdictio)  # Import mdJSON data dictionary as list path<-system.file("extdata", "e.g.dictionary2.json", package = "mdJSONdictio") input.json <- rjson::fromJSON(file = path)  # Translate list to data frame new.table<- mdJSONdictio::build.table(x = input.json, dictionary_num = 1, entity_num = 1)  # Export table to disk write.csv(x = new.table, file = "e.g.dictionary2.csv", na="", row.names = FALSE)

what: Import the mdJSON data dictionary to mdEditor
title: Next Steps in mdEditor
text: Open mdEditor browser navigate Import tab top page. Drag drop newly written mdJSON file click “Import Data.” generate Dictionary Record dictionary title specified function. may need refresh browser dictionary immediately appear.

what: Fix errors in the Dictionary Record
title: Next Steps in mdEditor
text: viewing Dictionary Record, notice orange exclamation mark next title indicates dictionary incomplete missing erroneous elements. Click orange exclamation mark view error details. errors can remedied completing Responsible Party field Main tab Entity Code Name Definition fields Entities tab. information fields, refer mdEditor User Manual. know dictionary free errors orange triangle disappears edit icon next Dictionary Record left-hand menu green. see three errors, make sure tabular data dictionary filled described Tabular Data Dictionary Template article.

what: Associate and maintain the Dictionary Record
title: Next Steps in mdEditor
text: Dictionary Record free errors, can associated Metadata record(s) describes (e.g. tabular dataset, shapefiles) help create archival quality project metadata. details process information, refer mdEditor User Manual. future updates Dictionary Record can done manually mdEditor web application.

title: Next Steps in mdEditor
text: Open mdEditor browser import mdJSON data dictionary file described Import mdJSON data dictionary mdEditor.Navigate Dictionary Record verify contents check errors. know dictionary free errors orange triangle disappears edit icon next Dictionary Record left-hand menu green. build.table() requires entity least one defined attribute, error-free Dictionary Record likely exported structure can processed function. Please note, Dictionary Record contains two entities, processed single tabular data dictionary.

title: Next Steps in mdEditor
text: Navigate Export tab top page. Make sure Metadata records, Contacts undesired Dictionaries selected (select deselect checkboxes search bars). Select desired dictionary click “Export Selected” right screen. Save file working space. prompted select save location, file may automatically saved local Downloads folder. intend translate one Dictionary Record table, can select export together single mdJSON file specify dictionary processed build.table() parameter dictionary_num.

what: Tools to Build and Transform mdEditor mdJSON Data Dictionaries
title: Tools to Build and Transform mdEditor mdJSON Data Dictionaries
text: Tools Build Translate mdEditor mdJSON Data Dictionaries

title: Tools to Build and Transform mdEditor mdJSON Data Dictionaries
text: can install released version mdJSONdictio GitHub : development version mdJSONdictio GitHub : detailed instructions troubleshooting tips, see Install mdJSONdictio R package.
code: # Install devtools from CRAN install.packages("devtools")  # Install mdJSONdictio from GitHub devtools::install_github("hdvincelette/mdJSONdictio", ref = 'v0.0.1') # Install devtools from CRAN install.packages("devtools")  # Install mdJSONdictio from GitHub devtools::install_github("hdvincelette/mdJSONdictio", ref = 'master')

title: Tools to Build and Transform mdEditor mdJSON Data Dictionaries
text: build.mdJSON() builds mdJSON data dictionary can imported mdEditor create Dictionary Record. input file must formatted tabular data dictionary template. build.table() builds tabular data dictionary mdEditor mdJSON data dictionary. mdJSON tabular data dictionaries can validated dataset validate.mdJSON() validate.table(), respectively.

title: Build mdJSON Data Dictionaries — build.mdJSON
text: Translates data frame tabular data dictionary list object can subsequently converted mdJSON imported mdEditor Dictionary Record. input tabular data dictionary must formatted template.

title: Build mdJSON Data Dictionaries — build.mdJSON
code: build.mdJSON(x, title)

title: Build mdJSON Data Dictionaries — build.mdJSON
text: x Data frame tabular data dictionary. title String designating title Dictionary Record mdEditor.

title: Build mdJSON Data Dictionaries — build.mdJSON
text: Returns list object corresponding tabular data dictionary.

title: Build mdJSON Data Dictionaries — build.mdJSON
code: # Import tabular data dictionary as data frame path<-system.file("extdata", "e.g.dictionary.xlsx", package = "mdJSONdictio") input.table<-readxl::read_excel(path = path)  # Translate data frame to list new.list<- mdJSONdictio::build.mdJSON(x = input.table, title = "Example Dictionary")  # Convert list to JSON new.json = rjson::toJSON(x = new.list)  # Export JSON to disk write(x = new.json, file = "e.g.dictionary.json")

title: Build Tabular Data Dictionaries — build.table
text: Translates list object converted mdEditor mdJSON data dictionary file data frame.

title: Build Tabular Data Dictionaries — build.table
code: build.table(x, entity_num, dictionary_num)

title: Build Tabular Data Dictionaries — build.table
text: x List object converted mdJSON data dictionary file. entity_num Default=1. Integer indicating entity one mdJSON file. dictionary_num Default=1. Integer indicating dictionary one mdJSON file (.e. multiple dictionaries exported together mdEditor). y Data frame dataset.

title: Build Tabular Data Dictionaries — build.table
text: Returns data frame corresponding mdJSON data dictionary.

title: Build Tabular Data Dictionaries — build.table
code: # Import mdJSON data dictionary as list path<-system.file("extdata", "e.g.dictionary2.json", package = "mdJSONdictio") input.json <- rjson::fromJSON(file = path)  # Translate list to data frame new.table<- mdJSONdictio::build.table(x = input.json, dictionary_num = 1, entity_num = 1)  # Export table to disk write.csv(x = new.table, file = "e.g.dictionary2.csv", na="", row.names = FALSE)

title: Validate mdJSON Data Dictionaries — validate.mdJSON
text: Compares mdJSON data dictionary dataset summarizes discrepancies data frame.

title: Validate mdJSON Data Dictionaries — validate.mdJSON
code: validate.mdJSON(x, y, dictionary_num = 1, entity_num = 1)

title: Validate mdJSON Data Dictionaries — validate.mdJSON
text: x List object converted mdJSON data dictionary file. y Data frame dataset. dictionary_num Default=1. Integer indicating dictionary one mdJSON file (.e. multiple dictionaries exported together mdEditor). entity_num Default=1. Integer indicating entity one mdJSON file.

title: Validate mdJSON Data Dictionaries — validate.mdJSON
text: Returns data frame comprised warning messages mdJSON data dictionary.

title: Validate mdJSON Data Dictionaries — validate.mdJSON
code: # Import mdJSON data dictionary as list path<-system.file("extdata", "e.g.dictionary2.json", package = "mdJSONdictio") input.dict <- rjson::fromJSON(file = path)  # Import dataset as data frame path<-system.file("extdata", "e.g.dataset.csv", package = "mdJSONdictio") input.data<-read.csv(path, na.strings = "", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # Validate dataset against dictionary all.warnings<- validate.mdJSON(input.dict, input.data, dictionary_num = 1, entity_num = 1)  # Export table to disk write.csv(x = all.warnings, file = "e.g.warnings2.json")

title: Validate Tabular Data Dictionaries — validate.table
text: Compares tabular data dictionary dataset summarizes discrepancies data frame. input data dictionary must formatted template.

title: Validate Tabular Data Dictionaries — validate.table
code: validate.table(x, y)

title: Validate Tabular Data Dictionaries — validate.table
text: x Data frame tabular data dictionary. y Data frame dataset.

title: Validate Tabular Data Dictionaries — validate.table
text: Returns data frame comprised warning messages tabular data dictionary.

title: Validate Tabular Data Dictionaries — validate.table
code: # Import tabular data dictionary as data frame path<-system.file("extdata", "e.g.dictionary.xlsx", package = "mdJSONdictio") input.dict<-readxl::read_excel(path = path)  # Import dataset as data frame path<-system.file("extdata", "e.g.dataset.csv", package = "mdJSONdictio") input.data<-read.csv(path, na.strings = "", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # Validate dataset against dictionary all.warnings<- validate.table(input.dict, input.data)  # Export table to disk write.csv(x = all.warnings, file = "e.g.warnings.json")
